name: RF Filter & Duplexer Integration
description: Sub-sector template for RF filter and duplexer/multiplexer integration patents. Covers duplexer design, quadplexer/hexaplexer topologies, filter bank architectures, and acoustic filter integration into RF front-end modules.
contextDescription: RF filters and duplexers are the highest-volume acoustic components in smartphones. A modern 5G smartphone contains 50-100 acoustic filters organized into duplexers and multiplexers. The trend toward carrier aggregation requires N-plexers (quadplexer, hexaplexer) that combine multiple duplexers. Key vendors: Murata, Skyworks, Qorvo, Qualcomm, Broadcom. Filter integration into RF front-end modules (FEM) is the dominant architecture trend — single module containing PA + filters + switch. Damages potential is very high due to per-device filter count.
scoringGuidance: 50-100 acoustic filters per smartphone — enormous per-device infringement volume.
Multiplexer (quadplexer, hexaplexer) patents are highest-value — enable carrier aggregation.
Filter bank architecture patents cover the organization of filters in the RF front-end.
Co-design of filter + PA in a single module (PAMiD) is the integration trend.
Isolation and rejection performance patents are critical for adjacent band operation.

questions.question: What filter integration topology does this patent address? Score 8-10 for novel multiplexer topologies (quadplexer, hexaplexer, octaplexer), 5-7 for duplexer design or filter bank architecture, 3-4 for basic filter integration, 1-2 for standalone filter.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Higher-order multiplexers are the trend for carrier aggregation — identify the topology.

questions.question: Does this patent address filter integration into RF front-end modules (PAMiD, diversity module, antenna tuning)? Score 8-10 for novel PA+filter co-design or multi-technology module (BAW+SAW), 5-7 for filter+switch integration, 3-4 for basic module packaging, 1-2 for no module aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Module integration reduces smartphone size and cost — dominant industry trend.

questions.question: Does this patent address filter/duplexer isolation or out-of-band rejection? Score 8-10 for novel isolation enhancement techniques (>55 dB), 5-7 for rejection optimization, 3-4 for general selectivity, 1-2 for no isolation focus.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Isolation is critical for CA — filters must prevent inter-band interference.

questions.question: Does this patent specifically enable carrier aggregation filter configurations? Score 8-10 for novel CA filter combinations (e.g., B7+B41+B66 quadplexer), 5-7 for CA-aware filter design, 3-4 for multi-band capability, 1-2 for single-band only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: CA drives the need for multiplexers — identify specific band combinations.

questions.question: How detectable is this filter/duplexer innovation via physical analysis? Score 8-10 for module-level architecture visible in X-ray or decapsulation, 5-7 for filter topology via cross-section, 3-4 for material analysis, 1-2 for design methodology only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: RF module teardowns routinely reveal filter topology and integration approach.

questions.question: Does this patent address manufacturing yield or test challenges for integrated filters? Score 8-10 for novel manufacturing techniques or yield improvement, 5-7 for test methodology, 3-4 for general fabrication, 1-2 for no manufacturing focus.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Manufacturing patents create barriers to entry for filter production.
